state_model.claim_boundary: This file preserves durable baselines, authority, declared capabilities, and promoted evidence. It intentionally does not store the current GitHub head, active branch, open pull requests, pull-request state, or current CI status.

last_substantive_baseline.title: Host the demo API on the existing collector VM
last_substantive_baseline.claim_boundary: PR #53 retired the active Microsoft.Web/App Service path and established the collector-hosted API design, workflow, bounded What-If classifier, validation, rollback, and service-verification contract. It did not establish a successful readiness gate, ARM What-If, Azure deployment, TLS, API health, or frontend operation.

latest_promoted_repository_event.title: Normalize Azure public IP quota evidence
latest_promoted_repository_event.claim_boundary: The parser repair and regression tests were merged after replaying the failed run evidence. The merge does not prove that a fresh workflow dispatch occurred or that ARM What-If, deployment, TLS, API health, or frontend operation succeeded.

work_ownership_resolution.rule: Before any write, resolve the live default-branch head, working branch, open pull requests, ownership, and exact-head CI. Pull-request descriptions carry branch-specific scope while open; merged project files must not claim that their own branch or pull request remains active.

repository_capabilities.claim_boundary: Repository presence proves versioned implementation only. PR #53 declares the collector-hosted path and PR #54 repairs typed quota normalization; Azure and service evidence still determine deployment and operation.

bounded_authority_grants.authorization_source: Explicit user instruction after the read-only Azure planning boundary was stated
bounded_authority_grants.claim_boundary: This grant authorizes Azure reads and ProviderNoRbac planning for the existing-collector report-publication path only. It does not authorize the demo-backend/API workflow, deployment, collector changes, RBAC changes, report publication, guest commands, or any Azure mutation.

authority_defaults.rule: Authority is fail-closed unless a narrowly scoped bounded_authority_grant and current human instruction explicitly permit the operation.

deployment_state.claim_boundary: Promoted evidence includes a bounded demo-backend/API deployment attempt that reached ARM, left partial App Insights and storage resources, and failed on Microsoft.Web quota, plus a collector-hosted workflow dispatch that stopped at readiness before ARM validation or What-If. Backend VM existence is observed, but listeners and API service behavior are not verified. PR #54 repaired the collector quota parser; a fresh collector-hosted dispatch remains required.

latest_promoted_evidence.freshness_boundary: This evidence is an observation from 2026-07-23T19:07:31Z and must not be treated as current Azure state after later activity. PR #54 changes repository parsing only; it does not refresh Azure evidence.

live_state_resolution.repository: Query the live GitHub repository default branch and exact head at read time.
live_state_resolution.pull_requests: Query open pull requests, branch ownership, draft state, reviews, threads, and mergeability at read time.
live_state_resolution.ci: Query workflow runs and jobs for the exact commit under evaluation.
live_state_resolution.azure: Use fresh scoped Azure inventory, subscription and tenant context, cost, quota, policy, RBAC, ARM validation, What-If, deployment, guest, and service evidence.

report_publication.freshness_boundary: This evidence applies to the separate public-report publication architecture at its recorded time. It does not establish current demo-backend/API state or current Azure readiness.

collector_demo_api.freshness_boundary: This evidence is an observation from 2026-07-23T19:07:31Z and must not be treated as current Azure state after later activity. PR #54 changes repository parsing only; it does not refresh Azure evidence.
